cross_link_registry.description: Master cross-reference table linking Specific Relief Act 1963, Sale of Goods Act 1930, and Indian Contract Act 1872

cross_links.description: Contract of sale must satisfy all elements of valid contract under Contract Act §10 (free consent, competent parties, lawful consideration, lawful object)
cross_links.practical_note: Checklist: before drafting any sale contract, verify §10 ICA requirements first

cross_links.description: Goods perished before contract — both sections treat such a contract as void on grounds of mutual mistake as to existence of subject matter
cross_links.practical_note: In goods disputes, always check both §7 SOGA (specific rule) and §20 ICA (general rule)

cross_links.description: Agreement to sell specific goods that subsequently perish = specific application of frustration doctrine in §56 ICA
cross_links.practical_note: §8 SOGA is lex specialis for goods; §56 ICA is the general frustration doctrine

cross_links.description: Time of payment not a condition in sale contracts unless so intended (SOGA §11) vs §55 ICA where time may be essence
cross_links.practical_note: Critical distinction: §55 ICA makes time potentially of essence; §11 SOGA modifies this for payment in sale of goods

cross_links.description: Breach of condition in SOGA entitles buyer to repudiate — right to treat contract as discharged derives from §39 ICA
cross_links.practical_note: Pleadings: cite both SOGA §12 (conditions/warranties distinction) and ICA §39 (repudiation right)

cross_links.description: Active concealment of defects in goods by seller = fraud under §17 ICA — creates additional liability beyond §16 SOGA implied fitness warranty
cross_links.practical_note: Consumer/buyer remedy: plead both §16 SOGA (warranty breach) + §17 ICA (fraud) where seller concealed defects

cross_links.description: Concurrent conditions (payment vs delivery) in SOGA §32 mirrors §51 ICA on simultaneous performance of reciprocal promises
cross_links.practical_note: When party refuses concurrent performance — cite §51 ICA for general rule, §32 SOGA for goods-specific rule

cross_links.description: Breach of instalment delivery may allow whole contract repudiation under SOGA §38 — mechanism of repudiation is Contract Act §39
cross_links.practical_note: Instalment contracts: test severability under SOGA §38, then apply repudiation rules from ICA §39

cross_links.description: Damages for non-delivery = loss directly and naturally resulting from breach. SOGA §57 and ICA §73 use identical standard
cross_links.practical_note: In damages pleadings, cite ICA §73 for principle + SOGA §57 for the specific cause of action in sale of goods

cross_links.description: Specific performance of goods contract (SOGA §58) is enforced via machinery of Specific Relief Act — SRA §10 onwards
cross_links.practical_note: SOGA §58 is the cause of action; SRA §10 is the enforcement vehicle. Cite both in specific performance prayers

cross_links.description: SRA §14 may exclude specific performance where money damages adequate — courts must check before granting SOGA §58 relief
cross_links.practical_note: Defence argument: if goods easily available in market, SRA §14(a) bars specific performance under SOGA §58

cross_links.description: Damages for breach of warranty under SOGA §59 quantified per ICA §73 principles
cross_links.practical_note: Quantification step: measure diminution in value per §59 SOGA, assess per §73 ICA remoteness rule

cross_links.description: Anticipatory breach in SOGA §60 is specific application of ICA §39 repudiation doctrine to sale of goods
cross_links.practical_note: Anticipatory repudiation in goods contract: SOGA §60 (right to sue immediately) + ICA §39 (general repudiation principle)

cross_links.description: Special damages only if in reasonable contemplation of parties — SOGA §61 and ICA §73 both apply the Hadley v Baxendale remoteness test
cross_links.practical_note: Special damages claim in goods disputes: same two-limb Hadley test applies via both provisions

cross_links.description: Exclusion clauses in sale contracts (SOGA §62) cannot override §23 ICA — cannot use exclusion to achieve unlawful object
cross_links.practical_note: NDA/sale drafting: exclusion of implied terms valid per §62, but check against §23 ICA for unlawfulness

cross_links.description: Agent's authority in sale of goods (SOGA §65) governed by Contract Act §§182-238 for general agency principles
cross_links.practical_note: Sale through agents: SOGA §65 for goods-specific rules + ICA §§182-238 for all other agency questions

cross_links.description: SOGA 1930 repealed ICA §§76-123 — SOGA is the lex specialis for sale of goods carved out from Contract Act
cross_links.practical_note: Historical note: Contract Act §§76-123 are dead letters — SOGA 1930 governs sale of goods exclusively

cross_links.description: Contract must be valid under ICA §10 before specific performance can be granted under SRA §10
cross_links.practical_note: Pre-condition checklist for SRA relief: first confirm valid contract under ICA §10

cross_links.description: SRA §10 specific performance is granted when damages under ICA §73 are inadequate — courts always weigh both
cross_links.practical_note: Prayer drafting: seek specific performance under SRA §10 AND alternatively damages under ICA §73

cross_links.description: When specific performance denied under SRA §14, damages under ICA §73 become the primary remedy
cross_links.practical_note: Always plead damages in the alternative when seeking specific performance

cross_links.description: Determinable contracts excluded from SRA §14(e) overlap with frustrated contracts under ICA §56
cross_links.practical_note: If contract determinable at will, neither specific performance (SRA §14) nor obligations (ICA §56) may persist

cross_links.description: Plaintiff's own repudiation/breach under ICA §39 is a personal bar to specific performance under SRA §16
cross_links.practical_note: Defence: demonstrate plaintiff's anticipatory breach under ICA §39 to invoke personal bar in SRA §16

cross_links.description: Cost of substituted performance (SRA §20) recoverable as damages under ICA §73
cross_links.practical_note: Substituted performance notice (30 days) → incur cost → recover as damages per ICA §73

cross_links.description: Compensation awarded under SRA §21 follows ICA §73 principles — party placed in position as if contract performed
cross_links.practical_note: SRA §21 compensation and ICA §73 damages use same expectation-interest measure

cross_links.description: Liquidated damages under ICA §74 may also be awarded under SRA §21 in lieu of specific performance
cross_links.practical_note: If liquidated damages clause exists, it may displace ICA §73 assessment and be awarded under SRA §21

cross_links.description: ICA §74 penalty/liquidated damages clause does not per se bar specific performance — SRA §23 clarifies this
cross_links.practical_note: Where contract has penalty clause per ICA §74, do not assume specific performance is barred — see SRA §23

cross_links.description: After dismissal of specific performance suit (SRA §24), fresh suit for ICA §73 damages for same breach is barred
cross_links.practical_note: Always plead ICA §73 damages as alternative relief in specific performance suit — cannot file separate later

cross_links.description: Fraud as defined in ICA §17 is a ground for rectification of instrument under SRA §26
cross_links.practical_note: Rectification suit: prove fraud per ICA §17 definition to trigger SRA §26 remedy

cross_links.description: Bilateral mistake under ICA §20 is also a ground for rectification under SRA §26
cross_links.practical_note: Rectification: ICA §20 (bilateral mistake) → SRA §26 (rectification) are the paired provisions

cross_links.description: Voidability under ICA §19 (coercion/fraud/misrepresentation) enables cancellation of instrument under SRA §27
cross_links.practical_note: Cancellation suit: show voidability per ICA §§19/19A, then seek cancellation under SRA §27

cross_links.description: Obligation to restore benefits on cancellation (SRA §30) mirrors ICA §65 — restore advantages under void/voidable contract
cross_links.practical_note: When cancellation ordered per SRA, restoration of benefits governed by ICA §65

cross_links.description: Perpetual injunction (SRA §38) requires showing damages under ICA §73 would be inadequate
cross_links.practical_note: To get perpetual injunction, affirmatively argue ICA §73 damages are inadequate remedy

cross_links.description: Injunction to enforce contracts in restraint of trade (ICA §27) refused under SRA §41
cross_links.practical_note: Non-compete/restraint of trade clauses: cannot get injunction to enforce positive covenant per ICA §27 + SRA §41

cross_links.description: SRA §42 allows injunction on negative covenant even where positive covenant is void under ICA §27 restraint of trade
cross_links.practical_note: NDA/employment: enforce negative (non-disclosure) covenant via SRA §42 injunction even if non-compete positive part void under ICA §27

key_sections_for_nda_drafter.note: SRA §42 injunction available for breach of NDA negative covenant even if non-compete positive covenant void

key_sections_for_nda_drafter.scenario: Specific performance of NDA obligations (e.g. delivery of licensed IP)
key_sections_for_nda_drafter.note: Post-2018, specific performance is a right not discretion — but personal obligations still excluded under §14
NDA liquidated damages clause + SRA §21 compensation + ICA §73 loss — all may apply

key_sections_for_nda_drafter.scenario: Injunction to prevent ongoing breach of NDA
key_sections_for_nda_drafter.note: Perpetual or mandatory injunction; show ICA §73 damages inadequate for confidential information breach
